title: Robin Williams as Gendo
wikitext:
[]: Robin Williams as Gendo             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Aug 14, 2014 8:40 pm Five years ago, some guy said some thing. NAveryW wrote: Carl confirmed that Robin Williams did indeed audition to play Gendo in the dub and that his audition recording still exists somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Aug 14, 2014 9:45 pm It also would've been insanely distracting and maybe they thought it would be prohibitively expensive. You wouldn't be able to separate actor from the role, unless he did it under an alias and disguised his voice well enough. It would be interesting to have this uncovered and see what scene he read from.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 14, 2014 9:49 pm Relevant: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZJijaSv9pjs#t=14m55s

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Aug 14, 2014 9:56 pm Guy Nacks wrote: It also would've been insanely distracting.

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Thu Aug 14, 2014 10:00 pm Tumbling Down wrote: I don't know what that means.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Aug 14, 2014 10:46 pm Firetrout wrote: I think he means people would've went "Hey, it's that guy!" instead of paying attention to the scene.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Aug 14, 2014 11:16 pm ^ Not really. You just remove Spike Spencer and everyone else who isn’t in the Hollywood big leagues and you cast the English dub the same way Disney does for Ghibli movies. I’m picturing Robin Williams more as Fuyutsuki, with Patrick Stewart as Gendo. Ellie Fanning can play Asuka, and Scarlett Johansson can play either Misato or Ritsuko. Even if those are the only Hollywood players in the dub, we’ve already increased its quality 10 fold and made it so people aren’t constantly asking “Hey, is that voice from that one actor guy?"

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Aug 14, 2014 11:33 pm Firetrout wrote: I think he means people would've went "Hey, it's that guy!" instead of paying attention to the scene.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 15, 2014 1:04 am Probably the bigger problem would be the requirement to pay SAG wages rather than whatever pittance they wanted to offer the voice talent at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Aug 15, 2014 9:38 am I'm pretty sure Robin Williams could have played Asuka and it still would have been golden.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Aug 16, 2014 3:43 pm I'd bet Robin Williams would have played Gendo for free. It's not as if he needed the paycheck. Too bad...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Aug 16, 2014 3:46 pm BrikHaus wrote: I'd bet Robin Williams would have played Gendo for free. It's not as if he needed the paycheck. Too bad...

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Aug 16, 2014 3:57 pm Ornette wrote: Maybe back during the original dub, but by his second divorce, crippling alimony payments forced him to sell his house and pick up any job he could get, including returning to TV.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Aug 16, 2014 6:08 pm Personally, I'd love to hear it. The man was a talent: he could be dramatic, creepy, subtle, frightening, pathetic, heartbreaking, terrifying, electrifying...I would have loved to hear what he could have brought a character as complex as Gendo, especially considering how much he loved Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sat Aug 16, 2014 8:20 pm Perhaps it would have made Eva more of a mainstream success in the US when it first aired. Think about it--Robin Williams was huge in the 90s (Aladdin, Jumanji, even FernGully)--and having him voice a character in an anime would have given it a much-needed boost in the overseas market.

[]:             Quote Post by ApolloIVtv » Tue Aug 19, 2014 3:27 am I always thought, if Robin Williams was to at least get a cameo in an Evangelion movie (live action or anime) he should've play Keel. He would be about his age by then, and he sort of looks like him, but he could've just done the voice itself too. I'm sure Williams was capable of making and acting out a voice for someone who's already had a variety of colorful voices such as Chairman Keel.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Aug 19, 2014 3:32 am He would've made a great Fuyutsuki.

[]:             Quote Post by kuzronk » Wed Aug 20, 2014 11:54 pm I do want to hear this. If he was chosen for the dub the series would be more controversial though.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Aug 22, 2014 8:46 am kuzronk wrote: If he was chosen for the dub the series would be more controversial though.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 22, 2014 12:22 pm The show was controversial to begin with. Attaching a huge name like Robin Williams to the project is going to attract huge attention, and that will cause the controversy surrounding the show to increase.
summary:
[]: Robin Williams as Gendo             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Aug 14, 2014 8:40 pm Five years ago, some guy said some thing. NAveryW wrote: Carl confirmed that Robin Williams did indeed audition to play Gendo in the dub and that his audition recording still exists somewhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Aug 14, 2014 9:45 pm It also would've been insane
